newsSince: Daikin continued to push its data-center cooling franchise: new hyperscale/AI cooling products (AHR Expo 2026), the Multijet large-venue unit, a dedicated global data-center business unit, a JPM Industrials Conference US growth pitch, and a $163M R&D test-lab expansion at Plymouth, MN. No new discrete catalyst (earnings/guidance) since June 9; the DC-cooling story is increasingly consensus.

sources.label: Daikin Applied next-gen hyperscale DC cooling
Daikin invests in next-gen DC cooling lab (Data Centre Magazine)
Daikin DC cooling pivot / thermal-infrastructure thesis (Simply Wall St)

verdicts.rankRationale: Master under-owned-bottleneck list: cooling/thermal exposure is real but the DC-cooling narrative (FUSION30, Chilldyne, CDU, dedicated DC unit) is now consensus at a $42B cap. No new catalyst since June 9; residual alpha keeps eroding without justifying a rank cut. Mild negative delta, hold rank 5.
verdicts.bull: Global HVAC leader leveraged to a DC thermal market growing to ~$16.6B by 2030; liquid-cooling/CDU and largest-capacity chillers extend reach.
verdicts.neutral: Genuine bottleneck adjacency but the cooling thesis is widely owned at $42B cap; alpha residual is thinning.
verdicts.bear: Large-cap consensus name; data-center cooling now priced in; consumer/residential HVAC cyclicality dilutes the AI lever.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Hyperscale liquid-cooling design wins / DC unit order momentum
verdicts.primaryRisk: Consensus crowding; HVAC pricing and residential cycle
